title: Treasurer puts scandal-prone Big Four in ASIC’s crosshairs
full_text:
Welcome to The Australian’s rolling coverage of news from Canberra and around the country.
Angus Taylor has accused Anthony Albanese of laying the ground for a union veto power over artificial intelligence, suggesting he failed to address substantive issues around the rollout of the technology.
The Prime Minister yesterday delivered a u-turn on AI regulation, vowing to develop a world-leading framework of laws specifically targeting the technology, despite last year rejecting the need for a standalone AI act.
The Opposition Leader said Mr Albanese’s address to the University of Sydney on Wednesday was “full of motherhood statements”.
“All he’s done in practical terms is create an office in an office, more bureaucracy, an office inside his own office to deal with these things and all,” Mr Taylor told Nine.
“This is moving fast and we need a government that is across it, that is leading on it. And that’s not what we saw.”
Mr Taylor says Mr Albanese’s speech was designed to appease the unions, claiming the movement’s demands for a veto power over the technology would arise at Labor’s national conference next week.
Nationwide News Pty Ltd.

author: David Ross, Thomas Henry, Tia Clark
heading: Data Science and AI
